    1. EndGameDialog(Game, long, Runnable, Runnable)

Class EndGameDialog

java.lang.Object
com.vaadin.flow.component.Component
com.vaadin.flow.component.dialog.Dialog
de.dhbw.memory.view.dialog.EndGameDialog
All Implemented Interfaces:
com.vaadin.flow.component.AttachNotifier, com.vaadin.flow.component.DetachNotifier, com.vaadin.flow.component.HasComponents, com.vaadin.flow.component.HasElement, com.vaadin.flow.component.HasEnabled, com.vaadin.flow.component.HasSize, com.vaadin.flow.component.HasStyle, com.vaadin.flow.component.HasTheme, com.vaadin.flow.component.shared.HasThemeVariant<com.vaadin.flow.component.dialog.DialogVariant>, Serializable
public class EndGameDialog extends com.vaadin.flow.component.dialog.Dialog
End-of-game overlay: winner / tie headline, per-player stats, elapsed time, and two restart options.

Cannot be dismissed by clicking outside (the user actively chooses how to continue). Shares the theme="dhbw" overlay treatment with the other dialogs and adds a .stats-panel block for per-player rows plus a .stats-footer for time + total moves. The headline uses the brand gradient via .dialog-title-gradient to echo the start screen.

Author:
Markus Wenninger
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • EndGameDialog

      public EndGameDialog(Game game, long elapsedSeconds, Runnable onSameSettings, Runnable onNewSetup)
      Builds the end-of-game overlay for the given finished Game: headline (winner / tie / single-player completion), per-player stats panel, elapsed time and total moves, and the two restart buttons.
      Parameters:
      game - the finished game (used for winner detection + stats)
      elapsedSeconds - total play time
      onSameSettings - invoked when the user clicks "Same Settings" — should rebuild the board in place with identical config
      onNewSetup - invoked when the user clicks "New Setup" — typically navigates back to StartView
